texts.full_text: a prominent Islamic scholar of Bangladesh and Nayeb-e Ameer of Hefazat-e-Islam Bangladesh. Abdul Malek Halim is the pioneer of Qawmi Mohila (Female) Madrasa in Bangladesh and the founder of Al-Jamiatul Arabia Lil Baneena Wal Banaat Haildhar, the first Qawmi Madrasa having female branch. At present, Abdul Malek Halim is serving as the president of the Madrasa. Malek Hamil is also serving as the senior vice-chairman of Islami Oikya Jote. He is an ex-chairman of Nizam-e-Islam Party. Education Abdul Malek Halim pursued higher education from Al-Jamiah Al-Islamiah Patiya. Career Maulana Malek started his career as a teacher at a local madrasa after completing education. Later he went to Saudi Arabia and worked as an Imam at a Masjid there. He became inspired by Maulana Malek became inspired by the Saudi education system and the madrasa system there. texts.full_text: ionship title and a top seed in the WPIAL Class AAA playoffs. Mark Kaboly - Tribune-Review Behind a strong offensive line, Chase Winovich was a dual-threat for Thomas Jefferson. On one hand, his powerful running game made him a valuable asset to the team, showing glimpses of the kind of physical prowess that had garnered the attention of Titans coach Ray Braszo. On the other hand, his exceptional vision and poise in the pocket proved beneficial for managing the offense, transforming him into the quarterback's reliable back-up. The team's decision to keep their approach simple led them to put their trust in the hands of Winovich. He proved to be an asset throughout the season, not just against West Mifflin but against every opponent. His duel performance both rushing and passing made it difficult for opposing teams to predict his plays and prepare adequately for defense. This apparent advantage enabled Thomas Jefferson to clinch the Big Nine championship title for the second time in a row, marking a significant milestone for the program.
